meta.architecture_style: Full-stack monorepo with DDD backend (FastAPI/Python) and dual Next.js frontends (main dashboard + landing page)
meta.executive_summary: Archie is a code analysis and architecture documentation platform that ingests GitHub or local repositories, runs multi-phase AI analysis via Claude, and produces structured blueprints, intent layers, and agent config files. The backend is Python/FastAPI with domain-driven design, async task processing via ARQ/Redis, and dual Supabase/Postgres persistence. The main frontend is a Next.js dashboard for managing analyses and viewing results; the landing app is a separate Next.js site with 3D visuals. Communication between frontend and backend uses REST + SSE for streaming analysis progress.

file_placement_rules.description: One APIRouter file per domain feature
Orchestration services live in application layer
Pure domain models with no infra dependencies
Concrete repo implementations behind domain interfaces
Page-level feature containers composed from sub-components
Custom hooks wrapping service layer for data fetching

naming_conventions.description: Service classes PascalCase; files and methods snake_case
Abstract domain interfaces prefixed with I
All React components use PascalCase filename and export
React hooks prefixed with use, camelCase

architectural_style.title: Domain-Driven Design with Layered Architecture
architectural_style.chosen: Strict 4-layer DDD: api → application → domain → infrastructure
architectural_style.rationale: Isolates business logic from infra, enables swapping DB backends (Supabase vs Postgres) without touching services

key_decisions.title: Dual Database Backend via Adapter Pattern
key_decisions.chosen: Abstract DatabaseClient interface with PostgresAdapter (asyncpg) and SupabaseAdapter
key_decisions.rationale: Supports both managed Supabase and raw Postgres deployments from one codebase

key_decisions.title: Async Task Queue for Analysis Workflows
key_decisions.chosen: ARQ (Async Redis Queue) for long-running analysis jobs
key_decisions.rationale: Prevents HTTP timeout on multi-phase LLM analysis; enables progress streaming via SSE

key_decisions.title: Phased LLM Analysis with RAG Context
key_decisions.chosen: Multi-phase Claude API calls with pgvector RAG retrieval per phase
key_decisions.rationale: Reduces single request token size; retrieves relevant code per phase for accuracy

key_decisions.title: Monorepo with Separate Frontend Apps
key_decisions.chosen: backend/ + frontend/ + landing/ as independent apps in one repo
key_decisions.rationale: Shared deployment tooling; landing and dashboard have different tech requirements (Three.js vs dashboard UI)
alternatives_rejected: Single Next.js app with routes for both

key_decisions.title: MCP Integration for Claude Tool Use
key_decisions.chosen: Model Context Protocol server embedded in infrastructure layer
key_decisions.rationale: Exposes analysis resources and tools directly to Claude via MCP protocol

trade_offs.accept: ARQ worker requires Redis to be running for async analysis
trade_offs.benefit: Falls back to in-process execution if Redis unavailable (start-dev.py detects this)

trade_offs.accept: Dual DB adapter adds abstraction overhead
trade_offs.benefit: Seamless switch between Supabase and raw Postgres via DB_BACKEND env var

trade_offs.accept: Two separate Next.js apps increase build complexity
trade_offs.benefit: Landing page can use Tailwind v4 + Three.js without polluting dashboard dependencies

components.name: API Layer
components.responsibility: FastAPI routes, DTOs, error middleware, SSE streaming

key_interfaces.name: FastAPI APIRouter
key_interfaces.description: Per-domain router modules registered in app.py

components.name: Application Layer
components.responsibility: Orchestrates use cases; coordinates domain + infra; runs analysis workflows

key_interfaces.name: PhasedBlueprintGenerator
key_interfaces.description: Multi-phase LLM blueprint generation with streaming

components.name: Domain Layer
components.responsibility: Core entities, abstract interfaces, domain exceptions; no framework dependencies

components.name: Infrastructure Layer
components.responsibility: Concrete DB adapters, GitHub clients, RAG/embedding engines, storage, MCP, event bus

key_interfaces.name: PostgresAdapter / SupabaseAdapter
key_interfaces.description: Dual DatabaseClient implementations selected by db_factory

key_files.description: Factory selects Postgres or Supabase adapter
MCP server exposing tools/resources to Claude

components.name: Workers
components.responsibility: ARQ background job processing for long-running analyses

key_files.description: ARQ task definitions for analysis jobs

components.name: Frontend Dashboard
components.responsibility: Next.js dashboard for repo management, analysis results, settings, blueprint viewing

key_files.description: Blueprint viewer with TOC and Mermaid
Global auth state via React Context

components.name: Landing Page
components.responsibility: Marketing site with Three.js shader background and GSAP scroll animations

key_files.description: Landing homepage with hero and CTAs

contracts.interface_name: backend/src/domain/interfaces/database.py — DatabaseClient interface between infra and domain
contracts.description: backend/src/domain/interfaces/database.py — DatabaseClient interface between infra and domain

contracts.interface_name: backend/src/domain/interfaces/repositories.py — repository interfaces between app and infra layers
contracts.description: backend/src/domain/interfaces/repositories.py — repository interfaces between app and infra layers

contracts.interface_name: backend/src/api/dto/requests.py + responses.py — HTTP API contract between frontend and backend
contracts.description: backend/src/api/dto/requests.py + responses.py — HTTP API contract between frontend and backend

patterns.name: REST JSON (Frontend → Backend)
patterns.how_it_works: Axios in frontend/services/*.ts sends JWT Bearer token; FastAPI validates and routes to services

patterns.name: Server-Sent Events (SSE)
patterns.how_it_works: Backend streams analysis events via sse-starlette; frontend consumes via EventSource

patterns.name: ARQ Task Queue via Redis
patterns.how_it_works: API enqueues job to Redis; ARQ worker picks up and runs PhasedBlueprintGenerator; results saved to DB

patterns.name: Dependency Injection Container
patterns.when_to_use: Wiring services, repos, DB clients at startup
patterns.how_it_works: config/container.py creates singletons; FastAPI Depends() injects into route handlers

patterns.name: Domain Event Bus
patterns.how_it_works: Services publish AnalysisEvent objects to event_bus.py; subscribers persist events and trigger downstream actions

integrations.purpose: Multi-phase code analysis and blueprint generation
Repository metadata fetch, clone, branch/PR creation
Primary persistence; Supabase also provides Auth
ARQ task queue backend and session caching

pattern_selection_guide.pattern: POST REST → ARQ enqueue → worker runs PhasedBlueprintGenerator
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Analysis takes minutes; must not block HTTP thread
Avoids polling; backend pushes events as they are persisted

pattern_selection_guide.pattern: Inject DatabaseClient via DI container; use repository interface
pattern_selection_guide.rationale: Keeps services DB-agnostic; adapter swapped at runtime

where_to_put_code.new_api_endpoint: backend/src/api/routes/{domain}.py — add route, register in app.py

pattern_selection.stream_progress: SSE via sse-starlette in analyses route
pattern_selection.server_data_fetching: Custom hook in frontend/hooks/api/ calling frontend/services/

stack.name: FastAPI
stack.purpose: Async REST API, SSE, OpenAPI docs

stack.name: Anthropic SDK
stack.purpose: Claude API for phased code analysis

stack.name: Next.js
stack.purpose: Dashboard and landing page React framework

stack.name: Redis
stack.purpose: ARQ queue backend and session cache

templates.code:
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
router = APIRouter(prefix='/{domain}', tags=['{domain}'])
@router.get('/')
async def list_items(service=Depends(get_service)): ...

technology.project_structure:
archie/
├── start-dev.py          # Dev orchestrator
├── backend/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── main.py
│   │   ├── api/          # Routes, DTOs, middleware
│   │   ├── application/  # Services, agents
│   │   ├── domain/       # Entities, interfaces, exceptions
│   │   ├── infrastructure/ # Persistence, external, analysis, MCP
│   │   ├── workers/      # ARQ tasks
│   │   └── config/       # Settings, DI container
│   ├── tests/            # unit + integration
│   ├── migrations/       # SQL migration files
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   └── cloudbuild.yaml
├── frontend/             # Next.js dashboard (pages router)
│   ├── pages/
│   ├── components/
│   ├── hooks/api/
│   ├── services/
│   ├── context/
│   └── lib/
└── landing/              # Next.js marketing site (app router)
    ├── app/
    └── components/

frontend.framework: Next.js 16.1.6 (dashboard: pages router; landing: app router)
frontend.rendering_strategy: Dashboard: CSR with Next.js pages router; Landing: SSG with app router

ui_components.name: Shell
ui_components.description: Root layout with sidebar + main content area

ui_components.name: AnalysisView
ui_components.description: Analysis results orchestration — blueprint, delivery, diagrams

ui_components.name: BlueprintView
ui_components.description: Blueprint markdown viewer with TOC navigation

ui_components.name: DeliveryPanel
ui_components.description: Delivery artifact viewer — CLAUDE.md, cursor rules, hooks

ui_components.name: MermaidDiagram
ui_components.description: Renders Mermaid DSL strings as architecture diagrams

ui_components.name: ShaderBackground
ui_components.description: Three.js animated WebGL shader for landing page

state_management.approach: React Context for auth + custom hooks for server state + useState for local UI state

global_state.description: frontend/context/auth.tsx — AuthContext: user, token, login, logout, isAuthenticated

state_management.server_state: Custom hooks in frontend/hooks/api/ wrap frontend/services/*.ts (Axios); likely React Query for caching in useRepositoriesQuery
state_management.local_state: useState in view components for modals, form inputs, selected items
state_management.rationale: Auth needs app-wide context; server data is scoped to feature hooks; no Redux needed for this scope

data_fetching.name: useRepositoriesQuery
data_fetching.mechanism: Custom hook with likely React Query useQuery

data_fetching.name: SSE Event Stream
data_fetching.mechanism: EventSource or fetch with ReadableStream to /analyses/{id}/stream

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS utility classes + shadcn/ui primitives in frontend/components/ui/; cn() helper from frontend/lib/utils.ts for conditional class merging
key_conventions: All components are functional with hooks — no class components
Service layer (frontend/services/) handles all HTTP; hooks consume services
View components in frontend/components/views/ are page-level containers
UI primitives in frontend/components/ui/ are never modified directly (shadcn pattern)
Auth token injected via useAuth() hook from frontend/context/auth.tsx

developer_recipes.task: Add a new analysis capability / feature flag
steps: 1. Add new field to AnalysisSettings entity in domain/entities/analysis_settings.py
2. Update settings DTO in api/dto/requests.py and settings route in api/routes/settings.py
3. Add toggle UI in CapabilitiesSettingsView.tsx; update frontend/services/settings.ts to send new field

developer_recipes.task: Add a new background analysis task
steps: 1. Define async task function in backend/src/workers/tasks.py with ctx parameter
2. Register task in WorkerSettings.functions list in backend/src/workers/worker.py
3. Enqueue from analysis_service.py using arq queue.enqueue('task_name', **kwargs)

developer_recipes.task: Add a new REST endpoint with frontend integration
steps: 1. Create route handler in backend/src/api/routes/{domain}.py using APIRouter; add DTOs
2. Register router in backend/src/api/app.py include_router call
3. Add Axios call in frontend/services/{domain}.ts; create frontend/hooks/api/use{Domain}.ts hook consuming it

developer_recipes.task: Run and debug analysis pipeline locally
steps: 1. Ensure backend/.env.local and frontend/.env.local exist (copy from .env.example); set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, REDIS_URL, DB credentials
2. Start Redis via docker-compose or local install; run python start-dev.py to launch backend + ARQ worker + frontend
3. Trigger analysis via dashboard at localhost:{frontend_port}; watch worker logs for PhasedBlueprintGenerator phases

architecture_diagram:
graph TD
    FE[Frontend Dashboard\nNext.js pages/] -->|REST + SSE| API[API Layer\nbackend/src/api/]
    LAND[Landing Page\nlanding/app/] -->|static| PUB[Public Web]
    API -->|Depends| APP[Application Services\napplication/services/]
    APP -->|interfaces| DOM[Domain Layer\ndomain/entities+interfaces]
    APP -->|calls| INFRA[Infrastructure\ninfra/persistence+external]
    INFRA -->|PostgresAdapter| PG[(PostgreSQL\n+ pgvector)]
    INFRA -->|SupabaseAdapter| SB[(Supabase\nManaged DB+Auth)]
    APP -->|enqueue| WORKER[ARQ Workers\nworkers/tasks]
    WORKER -->|via Redis| REDIS[(Redis)]
    APP -->|Anthropic SDK| CLAUDE[Claude API]
    APP -->|GitHub API| GH[GitHub REST API]
    INFRA -->|MCP server| MCP[MCP Protocol\ninfra/mcp/]

pitfalls.description: If Redis is unavailable at startup, start-dev.py falls back to in-process analysis silently — but production deployments require Redis for task persistence. Lost tasks are not retried.
pitfalls.recommendation: Always verify Redis connection before deploying; check REDIS_URL in backend/.env.local; monitor Redis memory to prevent queue overflow

pitfalls.description: DB_BACKEND env var controls PostgresAdapter vs SupabaseAdapter selection in db_factory.py; wrong value silently uses wrong adapter, causing query failures.
pitfalls.recommendation: Explicitly set DB_BACKEND='postgres' or DB_BACKEND='supabase' in .env.local; run backend/tests/unit/infrastructure/test_db_factory.py to verify

pitfalls.description: start-dev.py checks prompts.json version against .prompts-version file and hard-exits if mismatched — running without ./setup.sh after a pull will block startup.
pitfalls.recommendation: Always run ./setup.sh after pulling changes that bump prompts.json version

pitfalls.description: SharedEmbedder loads full sentence-transformers model on first use (5-10s); vector column dimension must match model output (e.g. 384 for all-MiniLM-L6-v2) or inserts will fail silently.
pitfalls.recommendation: Use singleton pattern (already in shared_embedder.py); ensure vector(N) column dimension matches model in migrations/001_initial_setup.sql

pitfalls.description: SSE streams from /analyses/{id}/stream are long-lived; if the frontend component unmounts without closing the EventSource, connections leak and Redis/ARQ events accumulate.
pitfalls.recommendation: Always close EventSource in useEffect cleanup function in the consuming hook

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: Backend validates JWT via python-jose; Supabase Auth is the identity provider; frontend stores token in AuthContext and attaches as Bearer header via Axios interceptors in services.
implementation_guidelines.usage_example: const { user, login } = useAuth(); await login(email, password);
tips: Token expiry must be validated server-side on every request via FastAPI Depends(get_current_user)
Supabase Auth claims must align with custom JWT validation logic in auth.py

implementation_guidelines.capability: Phased AI Blueprint Generation with Streaming
implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: PhasedBlueprintGenerator calls Claude across multiple phases with RAG-retrieved code context per phase; progress_callback persists AnalysisEvent objects; frontend streams events via SSE.
tips: Use tiktoken to count tokens before each Claude call to avoid context window overflow
Prompt loader can be swapped: file-based PromptLoader or DatabasePromptLoader injected via constructor

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: db_factory.py reads DB_BACKEND env var and returns either PostgresAdapter (asyncpg pool) or SupabaseAdapter (PostgREST); all repositories receive DatabaseClient interface, never concrete adapter.
tips: Connection pool (_cached_pool) is initialized once; call shutdown_db() on app shutdown to release connections
Add HNSW index on pgvector columns for production similarity search performance

implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: SharedEmbedder generates code vectors; stored in pgvector columns; RAGRetriever performs cosine similarity search to fetch relevant code snippets for each LLM analysis phase.
tips: SharedEmbedder is a singleton — do not instantiate per-request
Vector dimension in DB column must match model output dimension exactly

implementation_guidelines.capability: MCP Server for Claude Tool Integration
implementation_guidelines.pattern_description: infrastructure/mcp/ exposes analysis resources and tools via Model Context Protocol; Claude can query blueprints and codebase maps directly through the MCP server endpoint.
implementation_guidelines.usage_example: MCP server registered at /mcp route; Claude calls tools defined in infrastructure/mcp/tools.py
tips: MCP tools and resources must be kept in sync with actual domain entity schemas

development_rules.rule: Always add Python dependencies to backend/requirements.txt with pinned minimum versions; never use poetry or pipenv — the project uses plain pip with venv at backend/.venv
Always use npm for frontend and landing dependencies; run npm install in the respective directory (frontend/ or landing/) — no yarn or pnpm detected
development_rules.source: frontend/package.json, landing/package.json, start-dev.py npm run dev invocation

development_rules.rule: Never place infrastructure imports (asyncpg, supabase SDK, anthropic) inside domain/entities/ or domain/interfaces/ — domain layer must stay framework-free
development_rules.source: backend/src/domain/interfaces/database.py (pure ABC, no imports from infra)

development_rules.rule: Always create backend/.env.local and frontend/.env.local from .env.example before running; start-dev.py will hard-exit if either is missing
Always run ./setup.sh after pulling commits that change prompts.json — start-dev.py validates prompts version against .prompts-version and refuses to start on mismatch
development_rules.source: start-dev.py version check block reading backend/prompts.json and backend/.prompts-version

development_rules.rule: Always write async tests using pytest-asyncio for any code touching asyncpg, ARQ, or Anthropic SDK; use unittest.mock.AsyncMock for async dependencies
Never push Docker images manually — backend/cloudbuild.yaml defines the full build/push/deploy pipeline to GCR and Cloud Run; trigger via GCP Cloud Build
Never modify files in frontend/components/ui/ directly — these are shadcn/radix primitives; extend by composing them in feature components under frontend/components/views/
development_rules.source: frontend/components/ui/ structure mirroring shadcn/ui generation pattern

deployment.runtime_environment: Google Cloud Platform — Cloud Run for backend API; Supabase for managed PostgreSQL; Redis on Docker (local) or managed Redis (production)
compute_services: Google Cloud Run — containerized FastAPI backend (backend/Dockerfile)
Vercel or self-hosted — Next.js frontend and landing (deployment target not specified in repo)
deployment.container_runtime: Docker — python:3.11-slim base image defined in backend/Dockerfile
deployment.orchestration: None (Cloud Run is fully managed; no Kubernetes)
deployment.serverless_functions: None — all API logic runs in the containerized FastAPI app on Cloud Run
ci_cd: Google Cloud Build — backend/cloudbuild.yaml: build Docker image → push to GCR (gcr.io) → deploy to Cloud Run
distribution: Docker image pushed to Google Container Registry (GCR)
Cloud Run pulls image and serves backend API
supporting_services: Redis 7-alpine — ARQ task queue (docker-compose for local dev)
deployment.environment_config: backend/.env.local (DB_BACKEND, REDIS_URL, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, PORT); frontend/.env.local (NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL)
